author: BAAI
short_description: Strong general-purpose multilingual embedding model for retrieval, clustering, and semantic search.
long_description: BAAI/bge-m3 is a strong default when you need one embedding model that works across multilingual retrieval, clustering, and semantic matching. It handles longer inputs than many older embedding models and is a reliable starting point for product search, knowledge search, and benchmark exploration.
demo_readme:
# BAAI/bge-m3

Demo entry bundled with the example.

Use this card when you want a strong multilingual retrieval baseline with long-context support.

author: Jina AI
short_description: Flexible multilingual embedding model that works well for semantic search and classification tasks.
long_description: jina-embeddings-v3 is useful when you want one model family for semantic search, classification-style similarity, and multilingual document understanding. It is a good fit for teams that care about broad language support and stable general-purpose retrieval quality.
demo_readme:
# jina-embeddings-v3

Demo entry bundled with the example.

This card represents a flexible multilingual search model with balanced retrieval and classification quality.

author: Mixedbread
short_description: Large English-focused embedding model with strong retrieval quality for product and document search.
long_description: mxbai-embed-large-v1 is a good fit for English-heavy product search, enterprise search, and retrieval evaluation. It is often chosen when teams want a high-quality embedding baseline that pairs naturally with Mixedbread rerankers.
demo_readme:
# mxbai-embed-large-v1

Demo entry bundled with the example.

This card stands in for a strong English retrieval model that pairs well with reranking pipelines.

author: intfloat
short_description: Popular embedding baseline for semantic search and query-document matching in English.
long_description: e5-large-v2 is a well-known embedding baseline that remains useful for semantic search, FAQ retrieval, and query-document matching. It is especially helpful when you want a familiar reference point while comparing newer embedding models.
demo_readme:
# e5-large-v2

Demo entry bundled with the example.

This card gives the local demo a familiar semantic-search baseline.
